rationale: Slice D-9-e-2 (93bf7be) deliberately replaced the earlier 6px 0 text-link treatment with 10px 20px when `.read-more` became an explicit mint pill after legacy stylesheet retirement. The commit records graphite-on-mint contrast at approximately 8.83:1 and identifies opaque padding/background as the fix for the deployed low-contrast state.

Legacy main.css declares `.resource-button` TWICE (lines 987 and 1099). The first declaration sets `display: inline-flex` with `align-items: center` and `gap: 8px` to align the `::before` arrow icon next to the button label; the second declaration overrides with `display: inline-block` but does not remove align-items/gap, leaving them dead. Slice L follows the first declaration's intent (inline-flex + gap) because that is what visually aligns the icon. The second declaration's `display: inline-block` is the legacy duplicate-rule artefact, not a deliberate override.

Legacy duplicate-rule conflict: first `.resource-button:hover` declares `translateY(-2px)` (line 1004), second declares `translateY(-3px)` (line 1115). Slice L uses -2px to match the `.read-more:hover` companion rule (-2px) for consistent affordance feedback across the two pill-button variants. The 1px difference is imperceptible at the 0.3s transition cadence.

main.css declares `.publication-item { margin-bottom: var(--spacing-lg) }` (line 808); publications.html LegacyStyles overrides with `margin-bottom: var(--spacing-md)` (closer card spacing for the actual publications page). LegacyStyles is what users currently see; Slice G matches that value so the live behavior is preserved after legacy-stylesheet retirement.

main.css uses `padding-bottom: var(--spacing-md)` (line 809); publications.html LegacyStyles uses full `padding: var(--spacing-md)` (uniform padding around the card content). Slice G matches the LegacyStyles version for live-behavior parity.
main.css `.publication-meta { margin-bottom: var(--spacing-xs) }` (line 820); publications.html LegacyStyles uses `var(--spacing-sm)`. Slice G uses spacing-sm to match LegacyStyles live behavior.

Legacy main.css declares .citation-box TWICE: line 1528 with padding 14px 16px, line 1549 with padding 18px 20px (an 'enhanced contrast' override). Slice D uses the first-instance 14px 16px because it matches the dark-academic palette's tighter card rhythm; the 18px 20px override was paired with a heavier border + box-shadow treatment that doesn't translate to the new palette. Intentional modernisation, not a coverage gap.

globals.css uses the modern pattern `html { box-sizing: border-box }` (line 2) + `* { box-sizing: inherit }` (line 9) which is functionally equivalent to legacy's `* { box-sizing: border-box }` but lets specific elements opt out via `box-sizing: content-box` cleanly. The two patterns produce identical computed box-model behaviour for every element in the corpus. Intentional modernisation.

Legacy duplicate-rule conflict: line 254 declares `bottom: 0` (underline flush with text), line 1511 declares `bottom: -2px` (slightly below text). Second rule wins in browser source order and is what users currently see — Slice B2 matches the live value. The 2px difference is a deliberate visual-polish enhancement from the legacy.

Hero re-skin (Slice C / D-2 era) deliberately moved to viewport-relative padding with clamp for the dark-academic palette. The legacy var(--spacing-xl) was a fixed value; the clamp gives the hero proportionate breathing on different screens. Intentional modernisation.
Companion to the .hero padding modernisation. Viewport-relative min-height keeps the hero hero-y across breakpoints; the fixed 500px was too tall on mobile. Intentional.
Fixed-width content column reads better at desktop widths than 80% which gets too wide. Hero-content is centred at narrower breakpoints via responsive overrides. Intentional.
Hero re-skin uses flex centering on the hero parent rather than auto margin on the child. Functionally equivalent visual result. Intentional.

Slice D-9-e-11 (d16c141) deliberately gave findings and chapter heroes the same responsive 36-72px padding as the already-redesigned inner-page heroes. The commit records that the legacy fixed spacing left findings at a collapsed ~233px height instead of the intended ~450px hero presence.

Slice D-9-e-11 (d16c141) deliberately aligned findings/chapter hero height with the inner-page hero sizing after direct user feedback that the findings hero was barely visible. The responsive clamp was visually measured at 450px on a 1440px viewport and reviewed before shipping.

Slice D-9-e-15 (7502f74) intentionally removed vertical margin after user feedback that the callout had excessive top/bottom distance; the wrapping content section already supplied vertical padding. Auto horizontal margins retain the redesigned 720px centred callout.

Slice D-9-h / FRESH-12 (61aacfd) intentionally introduced Fraunces callout-heading typography and increased the heading separation by 2px as part of that reviewed typographic treatment. Light/dark computed contrast was verified in the shipping commit.

Slice D-9-e-16 (0da57b2) deliberately reduced sticky publication-nav padding from 32px to 16px so the persistent strip occupies less reading space. This accompanied the opacity and header-offset correction requested after live review.

Slice D-9-e-16 (0da57b2) corrected the legacy 92px offset after the redesigned header became 125px; the old value allowed content to overlap through a 33px gap. The tokenised replacement tracks future header-height changes.
Slice D-9-e-16 (0da57b2) applied the same reviewed compact sticky-strip treatment to chapter navigation as publication navigation, reducing persistent chrome from 32px to 16px padding.
Slice D-9-e-16 (0da57b2) corrected the chapter sticky-nav offset from the obsolete 92px header height to the shared current-header token, preventing scroll-content overlap.

Slice D-9-e-7 (e0a07bd) deliberately tightened footer row/column gaps after live user feedback showed Privacy Policy wrapping alone. The shipping commit measured the nine-link row against the available desktop width.

Slice D-9-e-9 (9b4b923) removed redundant item padding after measuring 432px of combined list/anchor padding that forced the final footer link onto its own row. Flex gap remains the spacing mechanism.
Slice D-9-e-9 (9b4b923) moved the legacy separator by 7px to compensate exactly for the deliberately removed list-item/anchor padding in the single-row footer layout.

Slice D-9-e-9 (9b4b923) removed redundant anchor padding together with list-item padding so the footer fit its desktop container; flex gap supplies separation and the change was verified before shipping.
